metrics.narrative: The business problem is well established and quoted: Gavin Greer (2026-03-17) said procurement is 'fairly manual... if we could have written on paper, we'd probably still be there', with a standalone PO system that 'is literally just a way of raising a PO to get suppliers paid.' Scale is quantified: ~£180m spend last year and ~25,000 POs (Martin/buyer, 2026-06-15), ~350 total users, ~25 power users, ~100 supplier/risk users, ~200 contracts plus ~50 new per year. Zycus quantified the value lever (Anthony, 2026-03-17): Merlin Intake reduces sourcing-to-PO cycle time 'by up to around about 60%.' What is NOT yet quantified is Global Switch's own baseline cycle time, Gavin (2026-03-17): 'step one is what are our cycle times. So we don't have anything in place.' Converting the 60% claim into a Global-Switch-specific savings/ROI figure is the open move.

champion.narrative: Martin Loveday (Product Owner, platform team, reports to Chris Richards) is a strong, active champion who authored the RFP off the Zycus primer: he leads the requirement, runs the cadence with Zycus, openly shares competitive standing ('you're a front runner for us', 2026-06-15), volunteers the internal process and timelines, and coordinates the use-case pack for the final demo. Critically, he is the access path to power — Anna-Marie (budget) and Gavin (EB) both engage Zycus through him. CRM MEDDPICC 2.0 (2026-06-16) names Anna-Marie, Martin and Gavin as champions. The watch item: advocacy at finance/legal is newer (they joined on 2026-06-24 and asked for a slower walkthrough), so arm Martin to extend the champion base to those reviewers ahead of the award.

competition.narrative: Zycus is the clear front-runner. CRM MEDDPICC 2.0 (2026-06-16) names GEP and Coupa as competitors and records 'we have a better AI platform leading with Merlin Intake, in their words is the best UI they have seen.' On the calls the live competitive threat is lower-cost vendors with thin scope: Martin (2026-06-15) 'some are cheaper, but it looks like they've only got like 3 modules, which we don't think is gonna cover what we need', and at least one vendor 'hasn't even put in an actual implementation cost.' Zycus scored ~99.8% on the buyer's RFP (which Martin noted was a 'carbon copy' of the Zycus S2P primer per the Next Step log), giving a strong structural advantage. The real residual threat is not a named rival but the SteerCo fairness re-score plus 'do nothing/delay' — both neutralised by converting verbal preference to a dated formal award. The wedge to defend: full-suite breadth plus the Merlin Intake/agentic-AI UI advantage.

identify_pain.narrative: Pain is specific, multi-owner and quoted. Core pain (Gavin, 2026-03-17): fully manual, fragmented procurement on Excel/email with a PO system that only issues POs post-contract; 'no automated processes... step one is what are our cycle times.' Compliance/guidance pain (Martin, 2026-03-17): first-time and occasional buyers do not know the process — 'we want something that guides them through the process.' Supplier-management pressure (Gavin, 2026-03-17): information-security requirements over the last 12 months and ESG reporting drive a need for robust supplier lists, and sites have independently created fragmented supplier lists (2026-06-15). Legal-team capacity is thin (2026-04-07). Pain owners: Gavin Greer (procurement process), Martin Loveday (platform/adoption), Anna-Marie Ferguson (reporting/BI and budget).

paper_process.narrative: Contracting mechanics are well understood and this is the critical path. Martin (2026-06-15): Zycus MSA already shared with Anna-Marie; Global Switch has no in-house tech counsel and will use external counsel Shoosmiths, 'so long as the MSA isn't completely out of scope... it will go quite quickly, but it will take a couple of weeks.' Anthony framed the plan: redline 'first few weeks in July if we are selected', then '~two weeks to do the actual signing.' DocuSign is the e-signature standard and Global Switch's existing DocuSign contract is coming out of term (2026-05-27), so integration scope is agreed. Critical-path math: decision ~6 Jul + 2-3 week Shoosmiths redline + ~2 week signing pushes hard against the 30 Jul close and likely slips signature into August. The closer's flag: no one on the Zycus side yet owns compressing this — pre-staging the redline pack and offering a direct Zycus-legal-to-Shoosmiths call the moment the award lands is the single biggest lever on the date.

economic_buyer.narrative: Two economic/budget authorities are mapped and not contradicted by the calls. CRM MEDDPICC 2.0 (updated 2026-06-16) names Gavin Greer (Group Procurement Director) as economic buyer and Anna-Marie Ferguson (Director of Business Intelligence and Performance) as budget owner. The calls corroborate Ferguson holds the commercial mandate: Anthony, 2026-05-27, 'Anna-Marie's given me the indication of what your budget is, so we're gonna be getting to that mark', and Martin, 2026-06-15, deferred the commercial sign-off to her ('Anna's gonna maybe come back on that specifically'). Gavin actively controlled scope and pricing decisions on the 2026-05-27 sizing call. The one gap a closer flags: Zycus has NOT had direct commercial closure with Ferguson on the final number — Martin relays her position rather than her engaging Zycus directly. In a champion-authored tender the path to her runs through Martin, not around him.

decision_process.narrative: The process is clearly mapped from the call sequence and stated directly. Path: discovery (2026-03-17) -> deep functional demo (2026-04-07) -> connect with Anna-Marie (2026-05-11) -> commercial sizing (2026-05-27) -> RFP clarification + final use-case demo agreed (2026-06-15) -> final SteerCo demo with 12 buyer attendees including procurement, finance and legal (2026-06-24). Martin (2026-06-15): 'our last step after that is decision time' with 'decisions in the next two weeks' (i.e. ~2026-07-06). A SteerCo/steering team gives the award. Note Gavin's fairness check (Martin, 2026-06-15): 'we need to make sure all the others have the chance... where they haven't answered correctly, we just need to review it a little bit more' — verbal preference is not yet a formal award. The widening attendee list on 2026-06-24 (finance, legal, service delivery) signals the committee converging on a decision.

decision_criteria.narrative: Criteria are explicit and quoted across calls. Usability/adoption is the lead criterion: Martin (2026-03-17) 'the usability of the tool at Zycus looks really something our users would enjoy using... a key requirement is to make it easy for everyone to use.' Functionality breadth and cost are scored: Martin (2026-06-15) 'in terms of functionality and cost way above anyone else. Some are cheaper, but it looks like they've only got like 3 modules, which we don't think is gonna cover what we need.' The RFP was scored against a Zycus-templated primer and Zycus 'came out at like 99.8%.' The final demo (2026-06-24) tested three named use cases: user-guided requisition journey, contract lifecycle management (sign-off/redlining) for legal, and workflows/approvals configuration. Open criterion: non-procurement-user adoption, which finance/legal flagged needs a slower hands-on walkthrough.

ai_positioning_strength.summary: AI/agentic story is landing strongly: buyer praised Merlin Intake as the best UI they have seen, and the agentic sourcing/SOW generation and Teams-embedded intake were central to the demos. Products in scope include Merlin and Merlin Intake plus ANA.

items.quote: the usability of the tool at Zycus looks really something that I think our users would enjoy using... a key requirement.
items.criterion: Ease of use / adoption for all users

items.quote: in terms of functionality and cost way above anyone else.
There is a need for a slower walkthrough of the system to ensure that non-procurement users can understand the process and obtain the necessary approvals.
desire to be in a position by the end of the month, with some flexibility extending into July.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Strong alignment on usability, full-suite functionality, Teams-based intake and AI; the open exposure is non-procurement-user adoption and the tight contracting timeline.

hard.ais_why: Engaged across AI features—Merlin intake and 'Merlin risk radar' with '19 credible sources' and alerting; senior sponsor: 'I like the interface... I like the team's integration.'	Explicit AI-reliability concern tempers excitement: 'everybody that has worked with AI knows that sometimes it gives you an answer that is not actually correct, but is telling you it's correct.'	Strong commercial momentum (RFP out, proposal requested, budget aligned) but AI interest is feature-level, not a stated agentic mandate. Three transcripts—high confidence.
hard.next_step:
What We Covered Today

  • Merlin Intake & Agentic Sourcing — end-to-end RFP creation via Microsoft Teams, AI-generated SOWs, price benchmarking and supplier recommendations
  • iContract — metadata extraction, clause library, Instareview, bulk amendment (in beta, targeted ~6 months), obligation tracking and SearchGenie
  • Supplier Onboarding & iRisk — D&B/Moody's integrations, configurable onboarding questionnaires, supplier risk radar

Guided Walkthrough Session

Following your feedback, we'd love to set up a focused, hands-on session where your broader team can experience the guided buying journey, approval flows and end-to-end process first-hand. 

We'd like to propose the following options, for a 1-hour timeslot.

  • Monday 29 June, 4:00 – 5:00 PM
  • Tuesday 30 June, 11:00 AM – 1:00 PM


  • RFP call check reviewed, no change - Final demo on the 22nd - decision 2 weeks after that - we are preferred vendor - confirmed on call - timelines for contracting is July
  • Submitted RFP on the 3rd June - we will hear back- The responses should arrive no later than 3 weeks after receipt of the RFP

  • Pricing to be finialised 01/06
  • RFP recieved deadline 4th of June
  • RFP is a carbon copy of Zycus S2P Primer!
  • Meeting with Anna-Marie (CTO) went well - Confirmed we demo'd the best, our platform meet their needs and wants a partnership,believe's - we can meet those objective
